sections.section_name:
Software Requirements Specification (SRS)
NexaTest – SRS Analysis & Test Case Generation System

sections.content: 1.1 Purpose The purpose of NexaTest is to automatically analyze Software Requirement Specification (SRS) documents, extract requirements, and generate structured test cases using an automated pipeline and API services. 1.2 Scope NexaTest allows users to upload SRS documents (PDF), extract text, segment sections, identify functional requirements, and generate suggested test cases. The system is designed for QA teams, interns, and analysts to accelerate requirement understanding and test design. 1.3 Definitions SRS — Software Requirements Specification API — Application Programming Interface FR — Functional Requirement NFR — Non‑Functional Requirement

2.1 Product Perspective NexaTest is a backend API-driven system built with FastAPI and Python. It processes SRS documents and produces structured outputs for testing workflows. 2.2 Product Functions Upload SRS PDF documents Extract text from SRS files Segment SRS into logical sections Extract functional requirements Store extracted artifacts Generate sample test cases Provide API endpoints for automation 2.3 User Classes QA Engineers Test Analysts Developers Interns and Researchers 2.4 Operating Environment Python 3.x FastAPI Pytest Local file storage Virtual environmental support

3.1 SRS Upload The system allows users to upload SRS documents via an API endpoint. 3.2 Text Extraction The system should extract readable text from uploaded PDF files. 3.3 Section Segmentation The system shall divide SRS text into structured sections such as Introduction, Functional Requirements, and Non‑Functional Requirements. 3.4 Requirement Extraction The system shall identify and extract functional requirements from the SRS content. 3.5 Test Case Generation The system shall generate sample test cases from extracted requirements. 3.6 API Access The system should provide REST API endpoints for upload, requirement retrieval, and test generation.

FR‑1 The system shall accept SRS documents in PDF format. FR‑2 The system shall store uploaded SRS files. FR‑3 The system shall extract text from uploaded PDFs. FR‑4 The system shall segment SRS sections automatically. FR‑5 The system shall extract functional requirements. FR‑6 The system shall return requirements via API. FR‑7 The system shall generate test cases from requirements. FR‑8 The system shall return test cases in JSON format. FR‑9 The system shall support automated pytest validation endpoints.

NFR‑1 The system should respond to API requests within 2 seconds for small SRS files. NFR‑2 The system shall handle invalid file uploads safely. NFR‑3 The system shall be modular and maintainable. NFR‑4 The system shall support unit and API testing. NFR‑5 The system should run in a virtual environment without conflicts.

6.1 API Interface POST /upload-srs — Upload SRS file GET /requirements/{id} — Get extracted requirements POST /generate-tests/{id} — Generate test cases 6.2 File Interface PDF documents are accepted as input.
Database persistence ML-based requirement classification Advanced NLP extraction UI dashboard Test case export to Excel/Test tools

requirements: The system shall divide SRS text into structured sections such as Introduction, Functional Requirements, and Non‑Functional Requirements.
The system shall identify and extract functional requirements from the SRS content.
The system shall generate sample test cases from extracted requirements.
